title: lotoxalis frutescens
intro: Lotoxalis frutescens, also known as Yellow Wood Sorrel, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Oxalis family, characterized by its yellow flowers and sour taste. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as a diuretic and to help reduce inflammation. The health benefits of Lotoxalis frutescens include its potential to treat kidney stones, urinary tract infections, and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of the plant are often used to make teas, tinctures, and salves, and it can be consumed as a fresh leafy green or used in cooking for its tangy flavor.
